coreMeaning.en: one who argues against a position for the sake of debate
coreMeaning.example: He played devil's advocate to challenge their assumptions.

memorySemantics.mnemonicExpr: devil + advocate → 故意唱反调的人

coreMeaning.cn: 偏僻的；弯曲的；不光明正大的
coreMeaning.en: deviating from a straight course; not straightforward; shifty
coreMeaning.example: He used devious methods to achieve his goals.

memorySemantics.mnemonicExpr: de(偏离)+via(路)+ous → 走歪路的 → 不诚实的

coreMeaning.en: in a devious manner
coreMeaning.example: He deviously manipulated the situation.

coreMeaning.en: the quality of being devious
coreMeaning.example: His deviousness was apparent to everyone.

coreMeaning.en: to plan or invent a complex procedure or system
coreMeaning.example: She devised a clever strategy to solve the problem.

memorySemantics.mnemonicExpr: de+vise(看) → 仔细看清楚 → 想出计划

coreMeaning.cn: 想出；策划；设计（devise的过去式）
coreMeaning.en: past tense of devise
coreMeaning.example: They devised a new plan.

coreMeaning.en: entirely lacking or free from
coreMeaning.example: The landscape was devoid of trees.

memorySemantics.mnemonicExpr: de(完全)+void(空) → 完全空的 → 缺乏

coreMeaning.en: the transfer of power from central to local government; degeneration
coreMeaning.example: The devolution of powers to regional governments was controversial.

memorySemantics.mnemonicExpr: de(向下)+volv(滚)+tion → 权力向下转移

coreMeaning.en: to give all one's time or resources to a particular activity
coreMeaning.example: She devoted her life to helping others.

memorySemantics.mnemonicExpr: de+vote(投票/誓言) → 全身心投入

coreMeaning.en: a person who is very enthusiastic about a pursuit or person
coreMeaning.example: He is a devotee of classical music.

coreMeaning.cn: 献身；奉献；忠实；热爱；虔诚
coreMeaning.en: love, loyalty, or enthusiasm for a person or activity
coreMeaning.example: Her devotion to her family was admirable.

coreMeaning.en: of or used in religious worship
coreMeaning.example: They sang devotional songs at the service.

coreMeaning.cn: 狼吞虎咽地吃；挥霍；耗尽
coreMeaning.en: to eat something quickly and eagerly; to destroy or overcome as if by eating
coreMeaning.example: He devoured the book in one sitting.

coreMeaning.en: having or showing deep religious feeling or commitment
coreMeaning.example: She was a devout Christian who prayed daily.

coreMeaning.en: tiny drops of water that form on cool surfaces at night
coreMeaning.example: The morning dew glistened on the grass.

coreMeaning.en: a drop of dew
coreMeaning.example: Dewdrops sparkled in the morning sunlight.

coreMeaning.cn: 垂肉（牛颈下的皮肤褶皱）
coreMeaning.en: a fold of loose skin hanging from the neck of cattle or other animals
coreMeaning.example: The old bull had a large dewlap swinging as it walked.

coreMeaning.cn: 露点（空气中水蒸气凝结的温度）
coreMeaning.en: the temperature to which air must be cooled for dew to form
coreMeaning.example: The dew point rose with humidity.

coreMeaning.cn: 吸引；画；拖；引起；提取；打成平局
coreMeaning.en: to produce a picture by making lines; to pull or attract
coreMeaning.example: The event drew a large crowd.

coreMeaning.en: a disadvantage or problem
coreMeaning.example: The main drawback of the plan is its high cost.

memorySemantics.mnemonicExpr: draw(拉)+back(后) → 被拉回的 → 缺点

coreMeaning.en: a play for theater, radio or TV; exciting events
coreMeaning.example: The drama of the rescue kept everyone on edge.

memorySemantics.mnemonicExpr: drama → 希腊语行动 → 戏剧

coreMeaning.cn: 戏剧的；急剧的；引人注目的
coreMeaning.en: relating to drama; sudden and striking
coreMeaning.example: There was a dramatic improvement in her health.

memorySemantics.mnemonicExpr: drama + -ic → 戏剧性的，引人注目的

coreMeaning.en: in a striking or impressive way
coreMeaning.example: Sales increased dramatically last year.

coreMeaning.en: to adapt for theatrical performance; to exaggerate the significance of
coreMeaning.example: The novel was dramatized for television.

coreMeaning.en: to hang or cover loosely with fabric
coreMeaning.example: She draped the cloth over the chair.

coreMeaning.en: likely to have a strong or far-reaching effect; radical and extreme
coreMeaning.example: The company took drastic measures to cut costs.

coreMeaning.en: a current of cool air; the act of drinking
coreMeaning.example: There was a cold draught coming through the window.

memorySemantics.mnemonicExpr: draught → draft的英式拼写，穿堂风

coreMeaning.cn: 通风良好的；有穿堂风的
coreMeaning.en: having air blowing through in a way that is cold and uncomfortable
coreMeaning.example: The old house was draughty in winter.

coreMeaning.en: a small amount; a unit of weight
coreMeaning.example: He poured a dram of whisky into the glass.

coreMeaning.en: past tense of drink
coreMeaning.example: She drank a cup of tea.

coreMeaning.en: a person who writes plays
coreMeaning.example: Shakespeare was the greatest English dramatist.

coreMeaning.en: the theory and practice of dramatic composition
coreMeaning.example: He studied dramaturgy at university.

memorySemantics.mnemonicExpr: drama + -urgy(工艺) → 戏剧创作理论

coreMeaning.en: a dealer in cloth and dry goods
coreMeaning.example: He worked as a draper's assistant.

coreMeaning.en: cloth or curtains hanging in loose folds; fabric goods
coreMeaning.example: Heavy drapery covered the windows.

coreMeaning.en: used to express mild annoyance
coreMeaning.example: Drat! I forgot my keys.

coreMeaning.en: to deprive of vitality or vital properties
coreMeaning.example: The disease devitalized the patient.

memorySemantics.mnemonicExpr: de(去除)+vital(生命)+ize → 使失去活力

coreMeaning.en: to transfer power or responsibility to a lower level; to degenerate
coreMeaning.example: The decision was devolved to local authorities.

memorySemantics.mnemonicExpr: de(向下)+volve(滚) → 权力向下移交

coreMeaning.cn: 深情的；忠诚的；专心致志的
coreMeaning.en: very loving or loyal; dedicated
coreMeaning.example: She was a devoted mother.

coreMeaning.en: having or showing deep religious feeling or commitment
coreMeaning.example: The devout congregation prayed together.

coreMeaning.cn: 德文郡（英格兰西南部郡）
coreMeaning.en: a county in southwest England
coreMeaning.example: They spent their holiday in Devon.

memorySemantics.mnemonicExpr: Devon → 德文郡，奶油茶发源地

coreMeaning.en: of the geologic period 419-359 million years ago; native of Devon
coreMeaning.example: Devonian rocks contain many fish fossils.

memorySemantics.mnemonicExpr: Devon + -ian → 泥盆纪（因德文郡地层命名）

coreMeaning.en: water droplets that condense on cool surfaces at night
coreMeaning.example: Morning dew covered the fields.

coreMeaning.en: a type of bramble bearing dark edible berries
coreMeaning.example: We picked dewberries along the hedgerow.

coreMeaning.en: a rudimentary inner digit on the foot of some dogs and cats
coreMeaning.example: The puppy had its dewclaws removed.

coreMeaning.en: in a dewy manner
coreMeaning.example: Her eyes looked dewily fresh.

coreMeaning.en: the state of being dewy
coreMeaning.example: The morning dewiness refreshed everyone.

coreMeaning.cn: 杜威十进制图书分类法
coreMeaning.en: a library classification system using numbers
coreMeaning.example: Libraries use the Dewey Decimal System to organize books.

memorySemantics.literal: 杜威十进位图书馆分类系统
memorySemantics.mnemonicExpr: Dewey(人名)+decimal(十进制)+system → 图书馆分类系统

coreMeaning.cn: 遗产受让人（法律：遗嘱中的受遗赠人）
coreMeaning.en: a person to whom property is bequeathed in a will
coreMeaning.example: The devisee inherited the estate.

memorySemantics.mnemonicExpr: devise(遗赠) + -ee(接受者) → 遗产受益人
